title: CI: make 055-rm parallel-safe
body:
Use safename, and add ci:parallel tags to all tests. (One
test was running "podman wait -l", which cannot work in
parallel. I choose to change it to "wait $cname", and
lose the -l testing)

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
Good question, and I only have a fuzzy answer: future added tests. I used `file_tags` in the `cp` tests because I find it hard to imagine someone adding a non-parallel-safe test there. Here, in `rm`, I can imagine an accidental `rm -a` or `-l` and I want everyone adding new tests to think very hard about each new test they add or review. Seeing `test_tags` repeated everywhere seems like a way to force people to think.

But it's not a very good reason and I have no evidence for it.
